programs.name: Stay NJ
programs.what_it_does: Stay NJ provides a property tax credit of up to 50% on property taxes, capped at $6,500, to encourage seniors to remain in New Jersey during retirement. It coordinates with existing programs like ANCHOR and Senior Freeze for additional relief.[2][7]
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners aged 65 and older with income up to $500,000, who reside in New Jersey full-time.[2][6]
programs.whats_unique: New program signed into law in 2024, effective 2026, specifically designed to retain seniors in NJ by countering high property taxes and 'snowbird' migration to lower-tax states.[2][6]
programs.benefit_value: Up to 50% property tax credit, capped at $6,500 annually.[2][7]

programs.name: Senior Freeze (Property Tax Reimbursement)
programs.what_it_does: This program reimburses eligible seniors for property tax increases on their principal residence above a base year amount. It protects homeowners from rising taxes as they age in place.[1][2][5]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ or those receiving Social Security disability, living full-time in their NJ home for 3 years, with income not exceeding $168,268 (adjusted annually).[2][5]
programs.whats_unique: NJ-specific freeze on tax increases via reimbursement, distinct from standard federal programs or common exemptions; coordinates with Stay NJ and ANCHOR.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Reimbursement for property tax increases over base year amount.[1][5]

programs.name: Jersey Assistance for Community Caregiving (JACC)
programs.what_it_does: JACC offers cost-shared in-home services, meal delivery, and caregiver respite to help seniors avoid nursing homes. It supports community-based care for those not qualifying for Medicaid.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors nursing home eligible but not Medicaid-eligible, typically low-income needing home-based support.[1][3]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded, non-Medicaid program unique to NJ for in-home services and respite, administered by Division of Aging Services.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Cost-shared home care services, meals, respite (value varies by need).[1][3]

programs.name: Pharmaceutical Assistance to the Aged & Disabled (PAAD)
programs.what_it_does: PAAD helps eligible seniors and disabled individuals save on prescription drug costs through a state-funded subsidy. It covers copays and premiums beyond federal programs.[5]
programs.who_its_for: NJ seniors 65+ and disabled persons meeting state income eligibility.[5]
programs.whats_unique: Purely state-funded NJ program providing direct prescription assistance, separate from federal Medicare Part D.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Significant savings on prescription drugs (exact amount varies by medications and copays).[5]

programs.name: Lifeline Credit
programs.what_it_does: Lifeline provides an annual credit to reduce gas and electric utility bills for low-income seniors. It eases energy cost burdens alongside programs like Universal Services Fund.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors who are PAAD or Medicaid beneficiaries.[1][3]
programs.whats_unique: NJ state program offering fixed annual utility credit, targeted at seniors beyond federal LIHEAP.[1][3]

programs.name: New Jersey Statewide Respite Care Program
programs.what_it_does: This program offers short-term or periodic breaks for family caregivers of frail elderly or impaired persons. It provides temporary relief to prevent caregiver burnout.[1][8]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of functionally impaired seniors in NJ.[8]
programs.whats_unique: Statewide NJ-funded respite specifically for elderly caregivers, coordinated through Division of Aging Services.[1][8]
programs.benefit_value: Temporary caregiver respite services (duration and cost coverage varies).[1][8]

programs.name: Alzheimer’s Adult Day Services Program
programs.what_it_does: This program subsidizes out-of-pocket costs for adult day care services for those with Alzheimer’s or related dementia. It supports participants at approved providers.[3]
programs.who_its_for: NJ residents with Alzheimer’s/dementia meeting eligibility, using participating adult day providers.[3]
programs.whats_unique: NJ-specific state subsidy for dementia adult day care, not a standard federal offering.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Partial subsidy on adult day care costs (amount varies).[3]

programs.name: Senior Citizen E-ZPass Discount
programs.what_it_does: Offers a discount on tolls for seniors using E-ZPass on NJ Turnpike and Garden State Parkway during off-peak times. It reduces transportation costs for older drivers.[3]
programs.whats_unique: NJ-only toll discount program tailored for seniors on state highways.[3]
programs.benefit_value: 10% discount on tolls during weekday off-peak and weekends.[3]
